title: dryopteris lamprocaulos
intro: Dryopteris lamprocaulos, also known as the golden shield fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae, typically found in the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Dryopteris lamprocaulos include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and protect against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
